Hi Le yao & Ritu,

The reason to have "download_run_once: true" is that installer VM act as the jump server to install all the components and used to push the images from the installer VM to other VMs. In case of containerization, we make it false, because container don't have space like VM to store the images and push to other machines. 

Current CI failed for reason https://github.com/kubernetes-sigs/kubespray/pull/4448/files, I also see the issues related to https://github.com/kubernetes-sigs/kubespray/commit/23c9071c3088fd58a781f6921205835f1a5d63eb

In both issues "download_run_once" made "false" and again changed to "true". In the Master Vagrantfile, https://github.com/kubernetes-sigs/kubespray/blob/master/Vagrantfile#L188-#L205, we having couple of useful command related to download, and how increase the provisioning, just checked them in Kubespray/Vagrantfile

Can we keep the following settings ?
	"download_run_once": "True",
        "download_localhost": "False",
        "download_cache_dir": ENV['HOME'] + "/kubespray_cache",
        # Make kubespray cache even when download_run_once is false
        "download_force_cache": "True",
        # Keeping the cache on the nodes can improve provisioning speed while debugging kubespray
        "download_keep_remote_cache": "False",

If the CI is failing again, if the "download_run_once": "True". Then we can have ci folder in kud/hosting_providers and run only that vagrantfile for CI system, let capture it different ticket for the patch.
Meanwhile, I will see how much time difference it taking for"download_run_once": "True" and "download_run_once": "false" in a normal setup.

Is this solution ok for you ?

if this is set to true, uses the localhost for download_run_once mode
(requires docker and sudo to access docker). You may want this option for
local caching of docker images or for Container Linux by CoreOS cluster nodes.
Otherwise, uses the first node in the kube-master group to store images
in the download_run_once mode
